response.justification: Bogdan has strong distributed systems and backend engineering credentials, and his recent Stripe work touches AI-agent infrastructure. However, the role requires an executive CTO who has demonstrably scaled engineering teams to 30+ people, architected production multi-agent AI systems at scale, and operated in regulated fintech environments. His resume reads as a technically excellent Staff/Principal engineer—deep backend expertise, polyglot technical range—but with no evidence of people leadership, team building, or regulatory domain experience essential for the CTO mandate. The team leadership gap is fundamental and disqualifying.
strengths: Exceptional distributed-systems and backend infrastructure expertise, including high-throughput microservices (Dunnhumby) and real-time systems
Recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent frameworks and MCP infrastructure at Stripe, showing active engagement with agentic AI tooling
15+ years of cross-domain technical depth across multiple languages and paradigms, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving in complex systems
concerns: No evidence of team leadership or people management—critical gap for a CTO role requiring demonstrated ability to scale engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people and develop senior technical leadership

Limited demonstrated experience in regulated financial environments or compliance-heavy domains; fintech exposure is secondary (RIDE Capital) without evidence of compliance/risk architecture ownership
Contributed to AI-agent tooling and frameworks but no demonstrated track record of architecting or operating production multi-agent decision systems end-to-end

key_factors.factor: Recent agentic AI and fintech exposure at Stripe and RIDE Capital
